volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Merchant (Section 5.2.890b - Section 5.2.930b)

sections.label: Section 5.2.890b
sections.translation: What if my feelings are agitated ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.891
sections.original: Ego istum in tranquillo quieto tuto sistam : ne time .
sections.translation: I ’ ll bring them for you into a safe and tranquil state ; don ’ t you fear .

sections.label: Section 5.2.892
sections.original: Obsecro te , loquere propere ubi sit , ubi eam videris .
sections.translation: Prithee ⋯ do say where she is — where you ’ ve seen her . Why are you mute ? Speak — you are torturing to death wretched me by your silence .

sections.label: Section 5.2.894
sections.original: Non longe hinc abest a nobis .
sections.translation: She isn ’ t far from here .

sections.label: Section 5.2.894b
sections.translation: Why then don ’ t you point her out , if you see her ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.895
sections.original: Non video hercle nunc , sed vidi modo .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I don ’ t see her at this moment ; but I saw her just now .

sections.label: Section 5.2.895b
sections.translation: Why , then , don ’ t you cause me to see her ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.896
sections.translation: I will cause it .

sections.label: Section 5.2.896b
sections.translation: That means a long time for one in love .

sections.label: Section 5.2.896c
sections.translation: Are you still in apprehension ? I ’ ll disclose it all . No person is there living more beloved by me than is he who has got her ; nor is there one to whom it is right that I should be a better wisher .

sections.label: Section 5.2.899
sections.original: Non curo istunc , de illa quaero .
sections.translation: I don ’ t care about that ; I ’ m looking for her .

sections.label: Section 5.2.899b
sections.original: De illa ergo ego dico tibi .
sections.translation: About her , then , I ’ m telling you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.900
sections.original: sane hoc non in mentem venit dudum , ut ubi sit dicerem .
sections.translation: Really , this has not come into my mind but this moment , to tell it you —

sections.label: Section 5.2.901
sections.translation: Tell me , then , where she is .

sections.label: Section 5.2.901b
sections.translation: In our house .

sections.label: Section 5.2.901c
sections.translation: If you are telling the truth , a worthy house , and aptly built , I deem it . But how am I to credit that ? Have you seen her ; or do you speak from hearsay ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.904
sections.translation: I ’ ve seen her myself .

sections.label: Section 5.2.904b
sections.translation: Who took her to your house ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.904c
sections.translation: Why , you ’ re asking an unfair question .

sections.label: Section 5.2.906
sections.original: quid tua refert , qui cum istac venerit ?
sections.translation: What matters it to you with whom she came ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.906b
sections.translation: So long as she ’ s there —

sections.label: Section 5.2.905
sections.translation: Are you telling the truth ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.905b
sections.original: Nil , Charine , te quidem quicquam pudet ;
sections.translation: Really , Charinus , you are not ashamed of anything .

sections.label: Section 5.2.907
sections.translation: She certainly is .

sections.label: Section 5.2.907b
sections.original: Opta ergo ob istunc nuntium quid vis tibi .
sections.translation: Then , for these tidings , do you wish whatever you please .

sections.label: Section 5.2.908
sections.translation: What if I do wish ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.908b
sections.original: Deos orato ut eius faciant copiam .
sections.translation: Pray to the Gods to bring its fulfilment .

sections.label: Section 5.2.909
sections.translation: You are laughing at me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.909b
sections.original: Servata res est demum , si illam videro .
sections.translation: My fortunes , in fine , are redeemed , if I can see her .

sections.label: Section 5.2.910
sections.original: sed quin ornatum hunc reicio ? heus , aliquis actutum huc foras
sections.translation: But why don ’ t I lay aside this garb ? ( Goes to the door of DEMIPHO ’ S house , and calls .) Hallo , somebody , come here this instant out of doors . Come out , and bring me thence a cloak this way .

sections.label: Section 5.2.911b
sections.translation: Well , now how much you do gratify me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.912
sections.original: Optume advenis , pueré , cape chlamydem atque istic sta ilico ,
sections.translation: You boy , who have come with such speed , take my scarf ( giving it ) , and now stand aside there ; that , if these things are not true , I may hasten to go upon this intended journey .

sections.label: Section 5.2.914
sections.translation: Don ’ t you believe me ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.914b
sections.original: Omnia equidem credo quae dicis mihi .
sections.translation: For my part , I really do believe everything that you tell me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.915
sections.original: sed quin intro ducis me ad eam , ut videam ?
sections.translation: But why don ’ t you introduce me to her , that I may see her ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.915b
sections.translation: Wait a little .

sections.label: Section 5.2.916
sections.translation: Why am I to wait ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.916b
sections.translation: It ’ s not a convenient moment to go into the house .

sections.label: Section 5.2.916c
sections.translation: You are torturing me to death .

sections.label: Section 5.2.917
sections.original: Non opus est , inquam , nunc intro te ire .
sections.translation: There ’ s no need , I tell you , for you to go into the house just now .

sections.label: Section 5.2.917b
sections.translation: Answer me-for what reason ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.918b
sections.translation: She ’ s not at leisure .

sections.label: Section 5.2.918c
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.918d
sections.translation: Because it isn ’ t convenient to her .

sections.label: Section 5.2.919
sections.original: Itane ? commodum illi non est , quae me amat , quam ego contra amo ?
sections.translation: Is it so ? Not convenient to her who loves me , and whom I love in return ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.920
sections.original: omnibus hic ludificatur me modis . ego stultior ,
sections.translation: He ’ s trifling with me in every way . I ’ m too foolish to believe him . He ’ s only delaying me . ( Turns to the BOY .) I ’ ll put on my scarf again .

sections.label: Section 5.2.922
sections.translation: Stop a little , and listen to this .

sections.label: Section 5.2.922b
sections.translation: You boy , take this cloak [*] , please .

sections.label: Section 5.2.923
sections.original: Mater irata est patri vehementer , quia scortum sibi
sections.translation: My mother ’ s dreadfully angry with my father , because he has brought into the house a harlot before her very eyes , while she was away in the country . She suspects that she ’ s his own mistress .

sections.label: Section 5.2.925b
sections.translation: I ’ ve taken up my belt [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.926
sections.translation: She ’ s now enquiring into this matter in-doors .

sections.label: Section 5.2.926b
sections.translation: Now my sword ’ s in hand .

sections.label: Section 5.2.927
sections.original: Nam si eo ted intro ducam —
sections.translation: But if I were now to introduce you ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.927b
sections.translation: I ’ ll take my bottle , and be off from here .

sections.label: Section 5.2.928
sections.translation: Stop , stop , Charinus !

sections.label: Section 5.2.928b
sections.translation: You are mistaken ; you can ’ t deceive me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.929
sections.translation: And , i ’ faith , I have no wish .

sections.label: Section 5.2.929b
sections.original: Quín tu ergo itiner exsequi meum me sinis ?
sections.translation: Why , then , don ’ t you allow me to proceed upon my journey ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.930
sections.translation: I won ’ t let you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.930b
sections.original: Egomet me moror . tu puere , abi hinc intro ocius .
sections.translation: I ’ m delaying myself . Boy , do you this instant be off hence in-doors .
